text:
# Profile

Sushin Bandha is a Purdue University AI student (B.S. Artificial Intelligence, Expected May 2027) with hands-on experience in product management, identity & access management (IAM), machine learning, and full-stack AI development. He is an NREMT-certified EMT with 56+ hours of clinical field experience across hospital ER and pre-hospital EMS environments.

Key accomplishments include:
- PSEG IAM internship (Summer 2025): Product owner for an enterprise IAM platform — achieved 99%-defect-free launch with 300+ UAT/SIT test cases, built an IAM scalability business case securing executive approval.
- PSEG automation internship (Summer 2024): Led a 25-person cross-functional intern team to ship a company-wide onboarding portal; built 5 PoC automation tools securing executive approval.
- BASF wine yield forecasting: time-series ML solution with 92% accuracy and an MLOps pipeline reducing model update time by 97% (2–3 days → 1–2 hours).
- AI portfolio platform: production Next.js + TypeScript site with RAG chatbot, in-house embeddings, vector search, and hardened security.
- NREMT certification (Apr 2026): 56+ clinical hours across IU Health ER rotation and TEAS field ride-along.

Sushin also works as a Student Security Patroller with the Purdue University Police Department and as an Event Supervisor for Purdue Athletics (Big Ten events, 10,000+ patrons).

Interests include AI/RAG systems, MLOps, data-driven product development, building secure intelligent systems, and emergency medicine. Tone: professional, friendly, and concise.

# Experience

## PSEG — Product Management Intern, Identity & Access Management (IAM) (Jun 2025 – Aug 2025)
- Served as product owner for an enterprise IAM platform, triaging and prioritizing a 50+ item backlog of security bug fixes and feature enhancements
- Achieved 99%-defect-free launch by designing and executing 300+ UAT and SIT test cases, validating end-to-end user workflows and access permission logic across business-critical systems
- Built a business case for a new IAM scalability solution — conducted market analysis and stakeholder user research using Excel and PowerPoint — securing executive approval and a documented growth roadmap
- Collaborated with security engineers, compliance leads, and business stakeholders using Agile practices to deliver iterative product improvements on schedule

## PSEG — Product Intern, Automation & Internal Tools (Jun 2024 – Aug 2024)
- Engineered and presented 5 Proof of Concept (PoC) automation tools to senior leadership, securing executive approval and budget allocation for full-scale product development
- Led a cross-functional team of 25 interns to define requirements, architect, and launch a company-wide employee onboarding portal — directly accelerating new-hire time-to-productivity
- Owned end-to-end development of a critical process automation tool using Python and Salesforce, transforming a multi-day manual workflow into a near-instant execution pipeline

## Purdue University Police Department — PSSP Student Security Patroller (Sep 2025 – Present)
- Conduct systematic campus security operations including facility inspections, interior sweeps, and unauthorized access identification — findings documented in Git-tracked compliance reports
- Coordinate emergency response and crowd management during university events alongside full-time officers, leveraging real-time communication tools to reduce incident response time
- Apply structured risk assessment frameworks to identify and mitigate environmental safety hazards, maintaining campus security compliance across academic and administrative assets

## Purdue Athletics — Event Supervisor, Event Operations & Security (Aug 2025 – Present)
- Supervised multi-location event operations for 10,000+ patron Big Ten athletic events, coordinating cross-site staff deployment and managing real-time communication across security, gate, and parking teams
- Directed cross-functional staff teams spanning multiple entry points and facility zones, delegating responsibilities, resolving personnel conflicts, and ensuring consistent service delivery
- Executed time-critical incident response decisions, coordinating with law enforcement and facility management to resolve safety incidents while maintaining zero disruption to event flow
- Applied data-driven resource allocation strategies based on lot capacity and permit-tier analytics, reducing entry/exit congestion by 60% at peak-attendance events

## BASF — ML Forecasting Project (Engagement)
- Developed a time-series forecasting system for wine varietal sales with ~92% accuracy, enabling data-driven inventory and marketing decisions
- Implemented an MLOps pipeline to reduce model update time from 2–3 days to 1–2 hours, significantly improving model iteration speed
- Engineered automated data processing workflows to handle wine sales data ingestion, cleaning, and feature engineering
- Built comprehensive model evaluation framework with performance metrics, validation testing, and error analysis
- Delivered production-ready forecasting solution with documented deployment process and model monitoring capabilities

## Purdue University Orientation Programs — BGR (Boiler Gold Rush) Team Leader (May 2023 - August 2024)
**Note: BGR stands for Boiler Gold Rush, Purdue University's orientation program.

## Purdue University Orientation Programs — BGR (Boiler Gold Rush) Team Leader (May 2023 - August 2024)
**Note: BGR stands for Boiler Gold Rush, Purdue University's orientation program. This is NOT a restaurant or burger joint.

ation Programs — BGR (Boiler Gold Rush) Team Leader (May 2023 - August 2024)
**Note: BGR stands for Boiler Gold Rush, Purdue University's orientation program. This is NOT a restaurant or burger joint.**

- Served as a Team Leader (TL) during Boiler Gold Rush (BGR), Purdue's orientation program, responsible for welcoming, mentoring, and supporting a cohort of 20–25 first-year students through orientation
- Led team of 5-8 fellow orientation leaders during high-volume orientation sessions, coordinating workflow and delegating tasks to ensure efficient program delivery and positive student experience
- Guided and coordinated student groups through events, campus navigation, and resources to accelerate acclimation and build community while fostering an inclusive and welcoming environment
- Trained and mentored new orientation volunteers on program procedures, facilitation techniques, and student support strategies, reducing onboarding time and improving team performance
- Coordinated closely with fellow Team Leaders to design and deliver a cohesive, immersive orientation experience across sessions, aligning activities and messaging for consistent student support
- Provided ongoing peer mentorship and informal advising — acting as a resource, friend, and advocate — helping students navigate academic, social, and personal challenges
- Collaborated with Orientation Programs staff to train volunteers and iterate on programming based on participant feedback, continuously improving the orientation experience
- Facilitated large group activities and small group discussions to build connections among new students and create a sense of belonging
- Resolved student concerns and logistical issues with professionalism and empathy, maintaining high participant satisfaction ratings

## Kumon — Tutor (June 2022 - August 2023)
- Taught mathematics and English to elementary and middle school students, customizing instruction to individual learning styles and pace to maximize comprehension
- Developed engaging lesson plans and assessments that improved student proficiency and test scores, tracking progress through regular evaluations
- Mentored struggling students through patient, one-on-one instruction and positive reinforcement, building confidence and fostering a growth mindset
- Communicated regularly with parents about student progress, challenges, and recommendations, creating a collaborative support system
- Adapted teaching methods based on student feedback and performance data, continuously refining instructional approach
- Created a welcoming and supportive learning environment that encouraged curiosity, persistence, and academic excellence

## Clinical Experience

### IU Health — Arnett Hospital | Hospital Clinical Rotation (Fall 2026, ~28 hours)
- Completed a 28-hour emergency department clinical rotation, observing and assisting with EMT functions within a hospital setting across live ER intake and patient care workflows
- Gained hands-on exposure to hospital-based emergency protocols, learning how EMT scope of practice and standard of care (SOC) differs between pre-hospital ambulance environments and in-hospital ER operations
- Supported clinical staff during patient assessment, triage support, and care coordination — developing adaptability across high-acuity, fast-paced medical environments

### TEAS — Tippecanoe Emergency Ambulance Service | Field Ride-Along Rotation (Fall 2026, ~28 hours)
- Completed a 28-hour field ride-along with the local Lafayette EMS system, responding to live emergency calls alongside certified EMTs and paramedics in a pre-hospital environment
- Observed and assisted with patient assessment, scene management, and emergency medical interventions across a range of medical and trauma calls
- Applied NREMT training in real-world field conditions, reinforcing clinical decision-making, patient communication, and protocol adherence under time-critical circumstances

## Personal — AI Conversational Portfolio (2025 - Present)
- Designed and shipped an AI-powered portfolio with embeddings, vector search, and a RAG chatbot to provide interactive visitor engagement
- Hardened production setup with security best practices including HSTS headers, strict Content Security Policy, and automated build validation
- Built modern, responsive UI with Next.

# Projects

## Wine Varietals Yield Forecasting (BASF)
- Description: Delivered ~92% accurate wine sales forecasts; built automated MLOps pipeline to cut updates from 2–3 days to 1–2 hours.
- Tags: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, MLOps, Time-Series Forecasting

## PSEG Onboarding Portal
- Description: Led cross-functional intern team; defined and launched company-wide onboarding site. Built PoC tools that secured executive approval.
- Tags: Product Management, Salesforce, Team Leadership, Automation

## Personal Portfolio Site
- Description: AI-powered conversational portfolio with in-house embeddings + vector search (RAG). Modern Next.js frontend, Tailwind + shadcn/ui, security hardening.
- Tags: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, AI/RAG, Product

## Process Automation Tool (PSEG)
- Description: End-to-end development of automation transforming multi-day manual processes into near-instant execution.
- Tags: Python, Automation, Salesforce, Business Process

- Team leadership and cross-functional coordination; outcome-oriented execution

- Business case development, market analysis, competitive assessment

# Hobbies & Interests

## Technology & Continuous Learning
**AI & Machine Learning Exploration**
- Actively experimenting with agentic AI systems and multi-agent frameworks to understand emerging architectural patterns
- Reading applied machine learning research papers and technical blogs to stay current with state-of-the-art techniques
- Testing and evaluating the latest AI model releases including GPT-4, Claude Sonnet, Gemini, and open-source alternatives to understand capabilities and limitations
- Building custom utilities and automation tools for personal productivity and daily workflow optimization

**Professional Development**
- Pursuing extracurricular programs and certifications focused on secure software development practices and AI safety
- Following technology releases and breakthrough announcements driving the AI industry forward, particularly in areas of retrieval-augmented generation, multimodal AI, and edge deployment
- Participating in online communities and technical forums to discuss emerging trends in machine learning engineering and MLOps

**Personal Projects**
- Developing small-scale proof-of-concept applications to experiment with new frameworks, libraries, and development patterns
- Contributing to open-source projects that align with interests in AI tooling, developer productivity, and system automation
- Maintaining personal knowledge base and documentation system to capture learnings and technical insights

## Recreation & Personal Life
**Sports & Entertainment**
- Avid NFL fan with primary allegiance to the Kansas City Chiefs, following team analytics and game strategy
- Gaming enthusiast with preference for story-driven and cooperative titles including Death Stranding: Director's Cut, Uncharted series, Overcooked 2, Unravel Two, Battlefield series, Super Mario Jamboree, and Mario Kart
- Regular participant in intramural sports leagues and recreational team activities with friends
- Enjoys streaming entertainment including latest shows and movies on Netflix, Hulu, and similar platforms

**Outdoor Activities & Social Engagement**
- Snowboarding enthusiast seeking mountain trips during winter season
- Enjoying hiking, picnics, and outdoor recreation to maintain work-life balance and spend quality time with friends and family
- Exploring new destinations through travel experiences including cruises, road trips, and cultural expeditions to broaden perspectives and learn about different communities

**Community & Social Connections**
- Maintaining strong social network through regular meetups and group activities
- Balancing professional ambitions with personal relationships and recreational pursuits
- Interest in emergency medical services and community health, leading to pursuit of EMT certification

# Contact

Always slowly work your way towards collecting this information by asking them their name when you first meet them etc, and before the conversation make sure to end with the following:

If you’d like to get in touch, please share:
- Name
- Email
- Company (optional)
- Message / purpose

You can submit this via the site’s Contact section. If asking something outside the known context, the chatbot will offer this contact fallback to avoid speculation.

# Curated Bios

This folder contains structured markdown documents used to represent Sushin Bandha’s background and story in a retrieval-friendly format.

## Contents
- 01-profile.md — High-level profile and focus areas
- 02-experience.md — Roles and impact bullets
- 03-projects.md — Highlight projects and technologies
- 04-skills.md — Skills grouped by category
- 05-certifications.md — Verified certifications
- 06-education.md — Institutions, programs, highlights
- 07-hobbies-interests.md — Personal interests (optional)
- 08-goals.md — Near-term and long-term goals
- 09-contact.md — Contact fallback guidance

## Usage
- Keep entries concise, outcome-focused, and verifiable.
- After edits, regenerate embeddings to refresh chatbot context.
- Avoid sensitive or speculative content; prefer authentic, concrete facts.
